title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: file captures complete design context writetfl R package, derived extended design conversation. Read writing code. Companion documents docs/: docs/DESIGN.md — rationale every decision docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — function hierarchy, data flow, internal contracts docs/DECISIONS.md — decision log alternatives considered docs/TESTING.md — test plan scope

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: writetfl provides two exported functions writing ggplot objects multi-page PDF files precise, composable page layout: export_fig_as_pdf() — opens PDF device, loops list pages, closes device export_figpage_to_pdf() — lays single page header, caption, figure, footnote, footer sections using grid viewports core design goal pixel-precise control whitespace PDF page, specifically supporting clinical/regulatory report aesthetics outer margins, annotation zones, figure areas must independently sized never overlap.

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: ... export_figpage_to_pdf() absorbs: - overlap_warn_mm (default 2) — near-miss threshold mm; NULL disables overlap detection ... export_fig_as_pdf() forwarded export_figpage_to_pdf().
code: export_fig_as_pdf(   x,   file,   pg_width  = 11,   pg_height = 8.5,   margins   = unit(c(t = 0.5, r = 0.5, b = 0.5, l = 0.5), "inches"),   page_num  = "Page {i} of {n}",   ... )  export_figpage_to_pdf(   x,   padding        = unit(0.2, "lines"),   header_left    = NULL,   header_center  = NULL,   header_right   = NULL,   caption        = NULL,   footnote       = NULL,   footer_left    = NULL,   footer_center  = NULL,   footer_right   = NULL,   gp             = gpar(),   header_rule    = FALSE,   footer_rule    = FALSE,   caption_just   = "left",   footnote_just  = "left",   min_figheight  = unit(3, "inches"),   preview        = FALSE,   ... )

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: x single ggplot object, wrap list(list(figure = x)) x list, element must list least $figure file must single character string ending ".pdf"; error otherwise

previous_headings: Key behavioral rules (implement exactly as specified)
title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: page , arguments merged precedence order (first wins): x[[]] named list elements (highest priority) Direct arguments passed via ... export_fig_as_pdf() export_figpage_to_pdf() defaults (lowest priority) page_num processed glue::glue("Page {} {n}") current page index n length(x). result placed footer_right footer_right absent step 1 2 merging. words, footer_right always beats page_num.

previous_headings: Key behavioral rules (implement exactly as specified)
title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: five vertical sections top--bottom order: section present elements non-NULL Padding (padding argument) inserted two vertically adjacent present sections section absent, space padding reserved Rules (enabled) drawn inside padding gap header↔︎caption footnote↔︎footer respectively; add height
code: header  (header_left + header_center + header_right) caption figure                          ← fills all remaining space footnote footer  (footer_left + footer_center + footer_right)

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: Accepts: NULL, single string, character vector. Returns: list(text = <single string>, nlines = <integer>) Character vector → collapsed "\n" Embedded "\n" → counted nlines NULL → list(text = NULL, nlines = 0L)

previous_headings: Key behavioral rules (implement exactly as specified)
title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: Primary: convertHeight(grobHeight(grob), "inches", valueOnly = TRUE) Fallback cross-check: nlines * convertHeight(stringHeight("M"), "inches", valueOnly = TRUE) Return max(primary, fallback) conservative estimate Must called target viewport active

previous_headings: Key behavioral rules (implement exactly as specified)
title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: Priority (highest lowest): 1. gp[[element]] e.g. gp$header_left 2. gp[[section]] e.g. gp$header 3. gp bare gpar() object 4. gpar() default Merge building lowest highest, overwriting field--field. helper merge_gpar(base, override) handles field-level merging.

previous_headings: Key behavioral rules (implement exactly as specified)
title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: header footer rows, check: - left_width + center_width/2 > 0.5 * vp_width → left encroaches center - right_width + center_width/2 > 0.5 * vp_width → right encroaches center - left_width + right_width > vp_width → left right overlap (center) Threshold behavior (controlled overlap_warn_mm): - True overlap (gap < 0) → collect error vector - Near-miss (gap ≥ 0 < overlap_warn_mm mm) → rlang::warn() - overlap_warn_mm = NULL → skip overlap detection entirely

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: Throughout layout computation, errors appended character vector. drawing occurs layout checks pass. end layout planning, errors exist: Warnings issued immediately via rlang::warn() detected.
code: rlang::abort(paste(errors, collapse = "\n"))

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: .exit(dev.(), add = TRUE) ensures device closes even page errors.
code: export_fig_as_pdf <- function(...) {   # validate first, before opening device   pdf(file, width = pg_width, height = pg_height)   on.exit(dev.off(), add = TRUE)   # loop }

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: export_figpage_to_pdf(..., preview = TRUE): - Calls grid.newpage() draws currently open device - open close device - Useful interactive layout tuning RStudio / Positron viewer

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: Every page uses two-level viewport stack pushed inside outer_vp: grid.text() / grid.draw() coordinates relative currently active viewport. precise viewport active draw call.
code: PDF page (root viewport, full page)   └── outer_vp         Inset by margins (t/r/b/l) from page edges.         This is the "hard" margin — nothing ever draws outside it.         Coordinates: npc (0,0) = bottom-left of content area.         │         ├── [annotations drawn here while outer_vp is active]         │   header section grobs         │   footer section grobs         │   rule grobs (in padding gaps)         │         └── figure_vp               Inset from outer_vp by the space consumed by               header + header_padding + caption + caption_padding (top)               and footnote_padding + footnote + footer_padding + footer (bottom).               The ggplot is printed here with newpage = FALSE.

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
code: writetfl/ ├── CLAUDE.md                         ← this file ├── DESCRIPTION ├── NAMESPACE                         ← generated by roxygen2 ├── R/ │   ├── export_fig_as_pdf.R           ← exported; roxygen2 docs │   ├── export_figpage_to_pdf.R       ← exported; roxygen2 docs │   ├── normalize.R                   ← normalize_text(), normalize_rule() │   ├── grob_builders.R               ← build_section_grobs(), build_text_grob() │   ├── measure.R                     ← measure_grob_height(), measure_section_heights(), │   │                                    measure_header_widths(), measure_footer_widths() │   ├── resolve_gp.R                  ← resolve_gp(), merge_gpar() │   ├── overlap.R                     ← check_overlap() │   ├── layout.R                      ← compute_figure_height(), check_figure_height() │   ├── draw.R                        ← draw_figure(), draw_header_section(), │   │                                    draw_caption_section(), draw_footnote_section(), │   │                                    draw_footer_section(), draw_rule() │   └── utils.R                       ← validate_file_arg(), coerce_x_to_pagelist(), │                                        build_page_args(), collect_errors() ├── tests/ │   ├── testthat.R │   └── testthat/ │       ├── test-normalize.R │       ├── test-resolve_gp.R │       ├── test-overlap.R │       ├── test-layout.R │       ├── test-validate.R │       └── test-integration.R └── docs/     ├── DESIGN.md     ├── ARCHITECTURE.md     ├── DECISIONS.md     └── TESTING.md

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: Implement order allow incremental testing: utils.R — validation coercion (grid dependencies, fully unit-testable) normalize.R — text rule normalization (grid dependencies) resolve_gp.R — gp merging (grid dependencies) grob_builders.R — depends grid, normalize, resolve_gp measure.R — depends grid, grob_builders overlap.R — depends measure layout.R — depends measure draw.R — depends export_figpage_to_pdf.R — assembles helpers export_fig_as_pdf.R — wraps export_figpage_to_pdf Tests — written alongside file Roxygen docs — written functions implemented

previous_headings: Typical use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: Pass one ggplot object directly. page number ("Page 1 1") added bottom-right corner automatically.
code: p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   labs(x = "Weight (1000 lb)", y = "Miles per gallon")  export_fig_as_pdf(p, file = "figure.pdf")

previous_headings: Typical use
what: A report with a header on every page
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: Supply shared annotations via ...; apply every page. report title goes top-left date top-right, separated figure full-width rule.
code: plots <- list(   list(figure = ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt,  mpg)) + geom_point() +                   labs(title = "Weight vs MPG")),   list(figure = ggplot(mtcars, aes(hp,  mpg)) + geom_point() +                   labs(title = "Horsepower vs MPG")),   list(figure = ggplot(mtcars, aes(disp, mpg)) + geom_point() +                   labs(title = "Displacement vs MPG")) )  export_fig_as_pdf(   plots,   file         = "report.pdf",   header_left  = "Fuel Economy Analysis",   header_right = format(Sys.Date(), "%d %b %Y"),   header_rule  = TRUE )

previous_headings: Typical use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: Put text differs per page inside page’s list element. Per-page values always override shared defaults supplied via ....
code: pages <- list(   list(     figure  = ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg))  + geom_point(),     caption  = "Figure 1. Weight is negatively associated with fuel efficiency.",     footnote = "n = 32 vehicles."   ),   list(     figure  = ggplot(mtcars, aes(hp, mpg))  + geom_point(),     caption  = "Figure 2. Higher horsepower predicts lower fuel efficiency.",     footnote = "Pearson r = -0.78."   ) )  export_fig_as_pdf(   pages,   file         = "annotated.pdf",   header_left  = "Internal Draft",   header_right = format(Sys.Date(), "%d %b %Y"),   header_rule  = TRUE,   footer_rule  = TRUE )

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: defaults landscape letter (11 × 8.5 ) half-inch margins sides. Override :
code: export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file      = "portrait.pdf",   pg_width  = 8.5,   pg_height = 11,   margins   = unit(c(t = 1, r = 0.75, b = 1, l = 0.75), "inches") )

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: default footer right reads "Page {} {n}". Supply glue template change format, NULL turn entirely. footer_right value — whether supplied via ... inside page list element — silently overrides page_num page.
code: # Custom format export_fig_as_pdf(plots, file = "numbered.pdf",   page_num = "{i}/{n}")  # No page numbers export_fig_as_pdf(plots, file = "no-numbers.pdf",   page_num = NULL) pages_custom <- list(   list(figure = p, footer_right = "Appendix A"),   # overrides page_num   list(figure = p)                                 # gets "Page 2 of 2" ) export_fig_as_pdf(pages_custom, file = "mixed.pdf")

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: header footer rows left, centre, right slots. combination can used; absent slots consume space.
code: export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file          = "full-header-footer.pdf",   header_left   = "Protocol XY-001",   header_center = "CONFIDENTIAL",   header_right  = "2026-03-21",   footer_left   = "Department of Statistics",   footer_center = "\u00a9 Acme Corp",   # © Acme Corp   footer_right  = "Page {i} of {n}" )

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: Pass character vector text argument; elements joined "\n". Alternatively, embed "\n" directly string. styles equivalent affect reserved section height automatically.
code: export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file     = "multiline.pdf",   caption  = c(     "Figure 1. Fuel efficiency declines with vehicle weight.",     "Data: Motor Trend (1974). Points represent individual models."   ),   footnote = "Abbreviations: mpg = miles per gallon; wt = weight (1000 lb).\nSource: mtcars dataset." )

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: header_rule draws line header row caption figure. footer_rule draws line figure footnote footer row. live inside padding gap add height.
code: # Full-width header rule, half-width footer rule export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file         = "rules.pdf",   header_left  = "Report Title",   footer_right = "Confidential",   header_rule  = TRUE,   footer_rule  = 0.5 )  # Custom grob: dashed rule dashed_rule <- linesGrob(   x   = unit(c(0, 1), "npc"),   y   = unit(c(0.5, 0.5), "npc"),   gp  = gpar(lty = "dashed", col = "gray60"),   name = "dashed" ) export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file        = "dashed-rule.pdf",   header_left = "Report Title",   header_rule = dashed_rule )

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: Pass single gpar() style text uniformly, named list section- element-level control. Resolution priority (highest wins): element → section → global.
code: # All annotation text at 10 pt export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file        = "gp-global.pdf",   header_left = "Report",   caption     = "Figure 1.",   gp          = gpar(fontsize = 10) ) # Section-level and element-level overrides export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file        = "gp-list.pdf",   header_left = "Protocol XY-001",   header_right = "2026-03-21",   caption     = "Figure 1. Results.",   footnote    = "Source: internal data.",   footer_right = "Page 1 of 1",   gp = list(     header       = gpar(fontsize = 11, fontface = "bold"),     header_right = gpar(fontsize =  9, col = "gray50"),  # overrides header size     caption      = gpar(fontsize =  9, fontface = "italic"),     footnote     = gpar(fontsize =  8),     footer       = gpar(fontsize =  8)   ) )

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
code: export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file          = "centred-caption.pdf",   caption       = "Figure 1. Centred caption text.",   footnote      = "Right-aligned footnote.",   caption_just  = "centre",   footnote_just = "right" )

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: padding sets vertical gap two adjacent present sections. Rules drawn midpoint gap. Increase breathing room decrease pack layout tightly.
code: export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file        = "tight.pdf",   header_left = "Header",   caption     = "Caption",   footnote    = "Footnote",   padding     = unit(0.08, "lines") )

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: min_figheight (default unit(3, "inches")) prevents figure squeezed unreadable size. computed figure height falls threshold sections placed, call errors informative message drawing occurs.
code: # Relax the guard for a very tall annotation stack export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file          = "tall-annotations.pdf",   pg_height     = 6,   header_left   = "Title",   caption       = paste(rep("Long caption. ", 20), collapse = ""),   min_figheight = unit(1.5, "inches") )

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: left right header footer text wide enough risk collision, writetfl warns errors automatically. Gap < 0 (true overlap) → error drawing. 0 ≤ gap < overlap_warn_mm mm → rlang::warn() (drawing still proceeds). overlap_warn_mm = NULL → detection disabled entirely.
code: # Tighten the warning threshold to catch moderate crowding early export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file            = "overlap-check.pdf",   header_left     = "A moderately long left header",   header_right    = "A moderately long right header",   overlap_warn_mm = 20 )  # Silence overlap detection for a layout you have manually verified export_fig_as_pdf(   p,   file            = "no-overlap-check.pdf",   header_left     = "Left",   header_right    = "Right",   overlap_warn_mm = NULL )

previous_headings: All features, roughly in order of use
title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: preview = TRUE calls grid.newpage() draws whatever device already open, without opening closing PDF. useful interactive inspection RStudio Positron. iterate quickly, call repeatedly layout looks right, switch export_fig_as_pdf() final PDF.
code: # In an interactive session: opens the viewer pane export_figpage_to_pdf(   x            = list(figure = p),   header_left  = "Preview",   caption      = "Figure 1. Draft layout.",   footer_right = "Page 1 of 1",   header_rule  = TRUE,   preview      = TRUE )

title: Exporting figures to PDF with writetfl
text: export_fig_as_pdf() used, argument can specified three levels. highest-priority value wins: page_num applied merging: populates footer_right footer_right remains NULL steps 1 2.

what: Standardized Table, Figure, and Listing Output for Clinical Trial Reporting
title: Standardized Table, Figure, and Listing Output for Clinical Trial Reporting
text: Standardized table, figure, listing output clinical trial reporting. writetfl produces multi-page PDF files ggplot2 figures precise, composable page layouts required clinical trial TFL deliverables regulatory submissions. page divided five vertical sections — header, caption, figure, footnote, footer — whose heights computed dynamically live font metrics figure always fills exactly remaining space. Nothing ever overlaps. package designed clinical, regulatory, technical reporting contexts outer margins, annotation zones, figure areas must independently sized reproducible across many pages. equally suitable setting demands consistent, pixel-precise page layout.

title: Standardized Table, Figure, and Listing Output for Clinical Trial Reporting
code: # Install from GitHub (requires remotes or pak) remotes::install_github("yourorg/writetfl")

title: Standardized Table, Figure, and Listing Output for Clinical Trial Reporting
text: multi-page report shared header per-page captions:
code: library(writetfl) library(ggplot2)  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   labs(x = "Weight (1000 lb)", y = "Miles per gallon")  # Single figure — "Page 1 of 1" added automatically export_fig_as_pdf(p, file = "figure.pdf") pages <- list(   list(     figure  = ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point(),     caption = "Figure 1. Weight is negatively associated with fuel efficiency.",     footnote = "n = 32 vehicles."   ),   list(     figure  = ggplot(mtcars, aes(hp, mpg)) + geom_point(),     caption = "Figure 2. Higher horsepower predicts lower fuel efficiency.",     footnote = "Pearson r = -0.78."   ) )  export_fig_as_pdf(   pages,   file         = "report.pdf",   header_left  = "Fuel Economy Analysis",   header_right = format(Sys.Date(), "%d %b %Y"),   header_rule  = TRUE,   footer_rule  = TRUE )

title: Standardized Table, Figure, and Listing Output for Clinical Trial Reporting
text: Absent sections padding gaps suppressed entirely — blank space reserved .

previous_headings: Key features
title: Standardized Table, Figure, and Listing Output for Clinical Trial Reporting
text: Arguments passed via ... export_fig_as_pdf() apply every page. element page’s list always wins shared default. Priority order (highest first): page list element → ... argument → function default.
code: export_fig_as_pdf(   pages,   file        = "report.pdf",   header_left = "Shared title",     # applies to all pages ...   # page list can override:  list(figure = p, header_left = "Override") )

previous_headings: Key features
title: Standardized Table, Figure, and Listing Output for Clinical Trial Reporting
text: page_num (default "Page {} {n}") populates footer_right unless footer_right value already set. Use glue template set NULL disable.
code: export_fig_as_pdf(plots, file = "report.pdf", page_num = "{i} / {n}") export_fig_as_pdf(plots, file = "report.pdf", page_num = NULL)

previous_headings: Key features
title: Standardized Table, Figure, and Listing Output for Clinical Trial Reporting
text: header_rule footer_rule draw line inside padding gap sections. accept FALSE (), TRUE (full-width), numeric fraction viewport width, custom linesGrob.
code: export_fig_as_pdf(p, file = "ruled.pdf",   header_left = "Title",   header_rule = TRUE,   footer_rule = 0.5          # half-width, centred )

text: Pass single gpar() style annotation text, named list section- element-level control. Resolution priority: element > section > global.

Results.",   gp = list(     header       = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11, fontface = "bold"),     header_right = grid::gpar(fontsize =  9, col = "gray50"),     caption      = grid::gpar(fontsize =  9, fontface = "italic"),     footer       = grid::gpar(fontsize =  8)   ) )

previous_headings: Key features
title: Standardized Table, Figure, and Listing Output for Clinical Trial Reporting
text: drawing occurs, writetfl validates layout reports problems : Overlap detection — left right header footer text collide, call errors. Near-misses within overlap_warn_mm millimetres (default 2) trigger warning; set overlap_warn_mm = NULL disable. Minimum figure height — figure squeezed min_figheight (default unit(3, "inches")), call errors computed minimum heights.

what: Export a list of figures to a multi-page PDF — export_fig_as_pdf
title: Export a list of figures to a multi-page PDF — export_fig_as_pdf
text: Opens PDF device, renders page using export_figpage_to_pdf(), closes device. Guarantees device closure via .exit() even error occurs rendering.

title: Export a list of figures to a multi-page PDF — export_fig_as_pdf
code: export_fig_as_pdf(   x,   file,   pg_width = 11,   pg_height = 8.5,   page_num = "Page {i} of {n}",   ... )

title: Export a list of figures to a multi-page PDF — export_fig_as_pdf
text: x single ggplot object, named list page specifications. page specification list required figure element (ggplot object) optional elements corresponding text arguments export_figpage_to_pdf(): header_left, header_center, header_right, caption, footnote, footer_left, footer_center, footer_right. Per-page list elements take precedence values supplied via .... file Path output PDF file. Must single character string ending ".pdf". pg_width Page width inches. pg_height Page height inches. page_num glue::glue() specification automatic page numbering, {} current page number {n} total number pages. Set NULL disable. ... Additional arguments passed export_figpage_to_pdf(). serve defaults pages overridden per-page list elements x.

title: Export a list of figures to a multi-page PDF — export_fig_as_pdf
text: normalized absolute path PDF file, returned invisibly.

title: Export a list of figures to a multi-page PDF — export_fig_as_pdf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2)  # Single plot p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() export_fig_as_pdf(p, "single.pdf")  # Multiple plots with per-page captions plots <- list(   list(figure = p, caption = "Weight vs MPG"),   list(figure = ggplot(mtcars, aes(hp, mpg)) + geom_point(),        caption = "Horsepower vs MPG") ) export_fig_as_pdf(plots, "report.pdf",   header_left  = "My Report",   header_right = format(Sys.Date()) ) } # }

what: Lay out and render a single figure page — export_figpage_to_pdf
title: Lay out and render a single figure page — export_figpage_to_pdf
text: Renders single page five vertical sections: header, caption, figure, footnote, footer. Section heights computed dynamically font metrics figure occupies remaining space. layout errors (overlapping elements, figure short) collected reported together drawing occurs.

title: Lay out and render a single figure page — export_figpage_to_pdf
code: export_figpage_to_pdf(   x,   padding = grid::unit(0.5, "lines"),   header_left = NULL,   header_center = NULL,   header_right = NULL,   caption = NULL,   footnote = NULL,   footer_left = NULL,   footer_center = NULL,   footer_right = NULL,   gp = grid::gpar(),   header_rule = FALSE,   footer_rule = FALSE,   caption_just = "left",   footnote_just = "left",   margins = grid::unit(c(t = 0.5, r = 0.5, b = 0.5, l = 0.5), "inches"),   min_figheight = grid::unit(3, "inches"),   page_i = NULL,   preview = FALSE,   ... )

title: Lay out and render a single figure page — export_figpage_to_pdf
text: x list required figure element (ggplot object) optional text elements: header_left, header_center, header_right, caption, footnote, footer_left, footer_center, footer_right. List elements take precedence corresponding direct arguments. padding Vertical space adjacent present sections, unit object. Separator rules (enabled) drawn midpoint gap consume additional space. header_left, header_center, header_right Header text. Accepts NULL, single string, character vector (collapsed "\\n"). Horizontal justification follows argument name (left/center/right). Vertically top-justified. Overridden x$header_left etc. caption Caption text header figure. Accepts NULL, single string, character vector. Full-width; justification controlled caption_just. Overridden x$caption. footnote Footnote text figure. Accepts NULL, single string, character vector. Full-width; justification controlled footnote_just. Overridden x$footnote. footer_left, footer_center, footer_right Footer text. Mirror header arguments. Vertically bottom-justified. Overridden x$footer_left etc. gp Typography specification. Accepts either single gpar() object applied text, named list section- element-level control. Resolution priority (highest first): element-level (e.g. gp$header_left), section-level (e.g. gp$header), global gpar(). Example:   header_rule Separator rule drawn header next section (caption figure), fitted within padding gap. Accepts: FALSE: rule TRUE: full-width rule numeric (0, 1]: rule spanning fraction viewport width, centered linesGrob: drawn -, centered vertically padding gap. footer_rule Separator rule last body section (footnote figure) footer. specification header_rule. caption_just Horizontal justification caption. footnote_just Horizontal justification footnote. margins Outer page margins unit vector elements t, r, b, l (top, right, bottom, left). min_figheight Minimum acceptable figure height unit object. error raised computed figure height falls value. page_i Integer page index, used prefix layout error messages "Page <>: ". Set automatically export_fig_as_pdf(); normally supplied calling function directly. preview Logical. TRUE, calls grid.newpage() draws currently open device without opening closing device. ... Additional arguments. Currently recognised: overlap_warn_mm: numeric threshold mm near-miss overlap warnings. Set NULL disable.
code: gp = list(   header        = gpar(fontsize = 11, fontface = "bold"),   header_right  = gpar(fontsize =  9, col = "gray50"),   caption       = gpar(fontsize =  9, fontface = "italic"),   footer        = gpar(fontsize =  8) )

title: Lay out and render a single figure page — export_figpage_to_pdf
text: Invisibly returns NULL.
